_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: To restore and maintain stability and alignment of the joint, if possible, with normal anatomy of the ankle mortise.
Optimize functional recovery and prevent post-traumatic arthritis.
If treating an ankle fracture with surgery, consider operating on the day of injury or the next day.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with indication for surgery
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected limb.

claim.label: Post op X-ray is labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) showing affected part.
Discharge Summary including follow up advice

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post-op X-ray / CT of Ankle shows the presence of implant?

common_queries: Post-op X-ray / CT of Ankle does not show the presence of implant.
Pre-authorization clinical notes are missing or do not clearly state the indication for surgery.
Pre-authorization X-ray is missing or not correctly labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right) for the affected limb.
Clinical photograph of affected part (pre-auth) is missing or unclear.
Post-procedure clinical photograph is missing or unclear.
Post-op X-ray is missing or not correctly labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right) for the affected part.
Detailed operative note is missing or lacks sufficient detail on surgical steps or implant details.
Discharge Summary is missing or does not include follow-up advice.

clinical_key_pointers: Pain, swelling, deformity of the ankle.
Drawer's test, inversion and eversion stress tests may be positive.
Note the color and condition of the skin.
Skin integrity, assessment of circulation and sensation, should be precisely documented at presentation.
